overview.description: Display a date range picker with two connected date inputs and a calendar pop-up
overview.purpose: Display a date range picker with two connected date inputs and a calendar pop-up
whenToUse: When you need to select a range of dates
whenNotToUse: When you only need to select a single date

examples.basic: <mc-date-range min="" max="" startofweek="1" locale="en-US" dayperiod="short" previouslabel="Previous month" nextlabel="Next month" customstyles="" yearcap="20" calendarposition="bottom-left" format="YYYY-MM-DD" placeholder="YYYY-MM-DD" autocomplete="on" variant="default" fit="medium" labelposition="top" width="auto" icon="" prefix="" name="checkin-date" fromlabel="From" tolabel="To" legend="" orientation="horizontal" value="{"from":null,"to":null}"></mc-date-range>

props.name: min
props.description: Define minimal selectable date, unlimited if empty (ISO format YYYY-MM-DD)

props.name: max
props.description: Define maximal selectable date, unlimited if empty (ISO format YYYY-MM-DD)

props.name: startofweek
props.description: Start of the week (0 = Sunday, 1 = Monday, ...)

props.name: locale
props.description: Locale to render the month/day names in. By default the component will use the users browser language.

props.name: dayperiod
props.description: Format for displaying weekday names in the calendar header.

props.name: previouslabel
props.description: An aria-label to use for the previous month navigation button.

props.name: nextlabel
props.description: An aria-label to use for the next month navigation button.

props.name: showweeknumbers
props.description: Whether to render week numbers.

props.name: showadjacentmonthdays
props.description: Whether to render days from the previous and next month.

props.name: customize
props.description: A set of dates to customize. Use to display day indicators or disable selectable days within selectable date range if `min` and `max` are specified. Check the examples for more details.

props.name: customstyles
props.description: Custom styles that can be used in date cells

props.name: yearcap
props.description: How many years backwards and forwards from the current one to render in a month-year picker

props.name: calendarposition
props.description: Specifies the position of the calendar in regards to the target element

props.name: format
props.description: Date format to be used to populate input field (default YYYY-MM-DD). Check The [day.js website](https://day.js.org/docs/en/display/format) for available formatting options

props.name: open
props.description: Whether to open the calendar on render.

props.name: usemask
props.description: Whether to use input mask on the input field. The mask will be based on the provided format YYYY-MM-DD by default.

props.name: hiddenlabel
props.description: If set to `true` or the attribute is just presented without any value, then the label will not appear, but will be used as `aria-label` only

props.name: placeholder
props.description: The `placeholder` attribute specifies a short hint that describes the expected value of a field (e.g. a sample value or a short description of the expected format)

props.name: autocomplete
props.description: The HTML autocomplete attribute lets web developers specify what if any permission the user agent has to provide automated assistance in filling out form field values. The full list of possible values for this attribute are listed on MDN: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Attributes/autocomplete P.S. Check Examples -> Password autocompletion story to see a live demo on enabling password autocompletion behavior.

props.name: loading
props.description: If set to `true`, it'll set the input state to loading and a loading spinner will be shown. The leading/trailing icon, suffix/prefix or clear button (if exists) will be replaced by the spinner.

props.name: autofocus
props.description: The autofocus global attribute is a Boolean attribute indicating that an element should be focused on page load, or when the <dialog> that it is part of is displayed.

props.name: variant
props.description: Description missing

props.name: fit
props.description: Description missing

props.name: labelposition
props.description: Position of the label. On small screens, label will always appear on top. See examples under input component on how to set label with and align text within the label.

props.name: width
props.description: The width of the inner input container in percentage. If not provided the width will be `auto`.

props.name: icon
props.description: Name of the icon

props.name: prefix
props.description: You can either render prefix or an icon on the same side of the input. Preferably, the prefix should not be longer than 5 characters.

props.name: clearbutton
props.description: Description missing

props.name: keepclearbuttonvisible
props.description: Clearbutton will be visible at all times when user typed something in the field (not only during input focus)

props.name: disabled
props.description: Description missing

props.name: readonly
props.description: Description missing

props.name: required
props.description: Description missing

props.name: name
props.description: Name is required if the component is used inside a `form` element

props.name: rangeUnderflow
props.description: `True` if the date is below the minimum. Check via `document.querySelector('mc-input-date').validity.rangeUnderflow` or the `invalid` event's `event.target.validity.rangeUnderflow` Pratical example here

props.name: rangeOverflow
props.description: `True` if the date is above the maximum. Check via `document.querySelector('mc-input-date').validity.rangeOverflow` or the `invalid` event's `event.target.validity.rangeOverflow` Pratical example here

props.name: fromlabel
props.description: The label associated with the `from` date input. If the `from` label is HTML content, refer to the examples for how to supply it using slots.

props.name: tolabel
props.description: The label associated with the `to` date input. If the `to` label is HTML content, refer to the examples for how to supply it using slots.

props.name: hiddenlegend
props.description: Hides the group legend. The legend text will still be used as aria-label

props.name: legend
props.description: Fieldset legend can be passed as a property or slot.

props.name: autolayoutdisabled
props.description: By default, on `small` screens (max-width: 640px), the component's `orientation` is switched to vertical, regardless of the set orientation. However, setting `disabledautolayout` to `true` prevents this and preserves the current `orientation` setting.

props.name: orientation
props.description: Description missing

props.name: value
props.description: The value object, where `to` and `from` stand in for the beginning and ending dates.

events.name: input
events.description: Fired when the character is entered.

events.name: inputdateselected
events.description: Fired only when date is selected in the calendar.

events.name: focus
events.description: Fired when an input in mc-date-range is focused.

events.name: blur
events.description: Fired when an input in mc-date-range is going out of focus.

events.name: keydown
events.description: Fired when a key on keydown is pressed.

slots.description: Use the from named slot, when you want to customize, or have control over the details in the "from" input. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
slots.name: from
slots.example: Label as HTML Hint as HTML Error message as HTML

slots.description: Use the to named slot, when you want to customize, or have control over the details in the "to" input. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
slots.name: to
slots.example: Label as HTML Hint as HTML Error message as HTML

slots.description: Use the legend named slot, when you want to pass the legend as HTML. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
slots.name: legend

types.name: IDateRangeValue
types.jsdoc: Represents the value's structure in the date range component.

members.name: clearbutton
members.description: Whether to display a clear button.

members.name: keepclearbuttonvisible
members.description: Keep clearbutton visiable at all times (not only during input focus)',

members.name: fromlabel
members.description:
The label associated with the 'from' date input.
It can also be supplied using the 'label' slot on
the mc-input-date passed as 'from' slot.

members.name: legend
members.description: Fieldset legend can be passed as a slot, or property.

members.name: tolabel
members.description:
The label associated with the 'to' date input.
It can also be supplied using the 'label' slot on
the mc-input-date passed as 'to' slot.

members.name: value
members.description: The value, where "to" and "from" stand in for the beginning and ending dates.

guidelines.title: Date Range
guidelines.description: Lets users pick a range of dates.

best-practice: Make it clear what kind of date format you are asking for e.g. _YYYY-MM-DD_ or _DD-MM-YYYY_
Use min and max dates to help prevent user error. The selectable date should be realistic for the context and application capabilities
Display only week numbers in the <PageLink path="/components/calendar">calendar</PageLink> popover when the context requires it and when users expect to see them by default.
For labels use natural language and use the terms that the user would expect.
The label should describe what to input and not how.
Don't use verbs like enter, add, input etc in the label.
Don't add a colon ( : ) at the end of the label.
Use the hint text if additional context and explanation are needed.
Error messages should be clear, concise and provide actionable remedies to resolve the errors.
orientation: By default, the component is responsive and adapts to the immediate container it is placed within, but it is also possible to force either horisontal or vertical orientation.

designPrinciples.title: Colour roles
designPrinciples.description: "Understand our different colour roles, the meaning behind their names, and how to use them."

primary: This is our main UI colour and will be the one that is reflected in the primary buttons, checkboxes, radio buttons, switches etc. meaning it will be the most prominent colour in many components and in your UI together with the neutral tones.
secondary: This is also referred to as the "brand" colour and will be reflected in the secondary button. This will be used to highlight the brand, while it's labelled as secondary, it can serve as the primary action. However, it's important not to overuse it. Use this colour to draw attention, use it for that “big primary” search button.
neutral: Neutral colours are present in nearly all components and serve as the foundation for any user interface. They are used in different aspects of a user interface like backgrounds, borders, and text colours. This palette contains both white and black, as well as all shades in between.
error: This refers to a shade of red, its main purpose is to communicate and highlight something "negative." For example, it indicates that you are performing a dangerous action, such as deleting your profile. However, it can also be used in other situations, such as displaying that a profile is busy, has unread notifications, stock values have decreased, you have an unpaid bill or you typed something wrong in an input field.
success: This refers to a shade of green, its main purpose is to communicate and highlight something "positive." For example, that your file was successfully uploaded or that your profile was updated. But it can also be used for ex. displaying an online/available status or that numbers have increased.
warning: This refers to a shade of yellow, its main purpose is to communicate and highlight a potential problem. For example, it may indicate that you have lost connection to the server or that your payment details need to be updated by a certain date. It can also be used for e.g. to display an "away" profile or a pending status.
info: This refers to a shade of blue, and its main purpose is to help and inform the user. For example, it can highlight a related feature that may be beneficial for the user to know about or to indicate that a new version is available. It can also be used for e.g. to signal that the user is currently on a "free" plan.

relatedGuidelines.title: Accessibility
relatedGuidelines.description: Improving the user experience for everyone.

what-is-accessibility?: Accessibility means that websites, tools and technologies are designed and developed in a way that makes it possible for people with disabilities to use them. Accessibility also benefits people without disabilities for example people with temporary disabilities or situational disabilities.
Accessibility helps people perceive, understand and interact with our digital products and it takes auditory, cognitive, neurological, physical, speech and visual limitations into consideration.

But accessibility is also a mindset and a change in the way we think and talk about digital products. It is a cultural change that cannot be made by one team only. We, therefore, urge all our colleagues as well as top management to get involved and share awareness about this very important topic. We need your support!
why-is-accessibility-important?: One of the main myths about accessibility is that it only concerns a small group of people with severe disabilities. This assumption is far from the reality and according to the World Health Organisation (WHO), more than 1 billion people are living with some level of impairment that affects the way they interact with digital products and access information online.
When our websites, apps and other digital products are designed badly, they create barriers that exclude people and make access to our offerings cumbersome and in some cases not possible at all.
being-inaccessible-is-a-problem: It is an ethical problem when we exclude certain groups of people from our products and services.

It is a legal problem, especially in the US where companies are being sued if they are not accessible, which could have financial consequences in terms of fines as well as affecting the brand negatively.

It is a big business problem - if we choose to exclude a very big group of potential customers because they have a disability.
When we design and build products with accessibility in mind, we not only open up our products and experiences for more people, but our products start to become more human - in the sense that they reflect the diversity of society and become much more flexible and robust to accommodate many different needs and use cases.

relatedGuidelines.title: Input masks
relatedGuidelines.description: A way of controlling how data is entered into an input field.

when-to-use: Use in fields with a specific expected format like Container Number, Document Reference Number, Postal Code, Phone Number, or Date and Time. An input mask allows you to constrain and group the information into the needed format, without impairing the user's ability to copy/paste or correct mistyping.
indicating-expected-format: While an input mask takes care of the correct formatting as the user types in the value, using the placeholder or hint text provides the user with additional context as to what is being asked of them.
using-the-placeholder-text: For example, placeholder text can function as a prompt for the user for what format is required.
using-the-hint-text: For example, when the format in the input field is a complex mix between different types of characters it is a good practice to provide a reference example as a hint text.
